accounts.quick_context: UK's second-largest grocer. 5-year Microsoft Azure AI partnership (May 2024). Full Cresta demo Apr '26 — Kieran McLoughlin validated 100% QM as #1 pain ('200 of 10,000 calls — drop in the ocean'). Genesys + Salesforce confirmed. Ops team demo agreed.

accounts.financials: FY2024/25: Revenue £33.3bn (+1.5% YoY) · Operating Profit £1,036m (+7.2%) · EBITDA £2,192m · 'Next Level Sainsbury's' 3-year strategy (Feb 2024) — 5th consecutive year of market share growth · £200m share buyback active

ai_initiatives.title: 5-Year Microsoft Azure AI Partnership (May 2024)
ai_initiatives.detail: Sainsbury's and Microsoft announced a 5-year strategic AI partnership. Scope: AI-guided colleague tools, personalised promotions via Nectar, enhanced online search, AI-powered forecasting. CTO Clodagh Moriarty: 'Our collaboration with Microsoft will accelerate our ambition to become the UK's leading AI-enabled grocer.'
ai_initiatives.source: J Sainsbury plc press release, May 16 2024
ai_initiatives.cresta_angle: Azure AI partnership = senior exec mandate. Cresta runs on Azure. Frame as: 'Extending the Microsoft AI partnership into the contact centre — the one area Azure hasn't yet touched.'

ai_initiatives.title: No CC AI initiative — complete whitespace
ai_initiatives.detail: Despite the Microsoft partnership, no AI agent coaching, real-time assist, or automated QM in the Sainsbury's CC. Kieran confirmed manual QA only (200 of 10,000 calls reviewed). The Microsoft investment has not reached the contact centre.
ai_initiatives.cresta_angle: 'You've invested in Microsoft AI across your business — here is what it looks like in the contact centre.'

recent_news.cresta_relevance: AI mandate at CTO level. Cresta extends this into the CC — the one area not yet touched.

accounts.champions: Kieran McLoughlin ★★ — validated 100% QM as #1 pain, agreed ops team demo. Viswa Settipalli ★ — confirmed Genesys + SF stack, framing Cresta in 3-year strategy.

accounts.cc_footprint: Multi-brand CC: grocery, Argos, Nectar, Tu, Habitat. Thousands of agents. Offshore (Philippines). Manual QA: 200 of every 10,000 calls (2% sample). Genesys = routing only, no AI layer. Agent desktop: 3+ systems causing high cognitive load.
accounts.regulatory_signals: Sainsbury's Bank exiting FCA-regulated core banking. No FCA enforcement on retail CC. Annual Report FY25 explicitly states 'automating contact centre processes' as strategic priority. ICO: no major penalties. Consumer Duty applies to remaining FS interactions.
accounts.competitors: Salesforce Agentforce (Azure partnership may push this). Microsoft Copilot for CC (via Azure — Cresta is more CC-specialist). NICE CXone (if Argos uses separate stack).
accounts.why_cresta_wins: Integrates natively on Genesys + Salesforce — no rip-and-replace. Woolworths AU ran identical stack with measurable results. AI-native for CC vs Agentforce (CRM extended). Forrester Wave Leader. 100% QM = the one thing Agentforce can't deliver.

AI Agent — WISMO and account queries. Immediate measurable containment.

messages.subject: Sainsbury's ops team demo + Woolworths case study
messages.body:
Kieran,

Great session last week — the 200-of-10,000 framing was exactly the kind of specificity that helps us make the case internally too.

Attaching the Woolworths AU case study — they ran Genesys + Salesforce exactly as you do, and the QA automation went live within 8 weeks. The ops team comparison will be useful context.

On the ops team demo — I can flex to whatever format works. Happy to come in or run it remotely. What does the week of [date] look like?

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi [name], it's Marcus from Cresta — we spoke with Kieran and Viswa last week about the QA gap. Kieran mentioned you'd be involved in the ops team review. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We work with retailers like Woolworths who run Genesys and Salesforce just like you. We sit on top of both and give you 100% call QA — replacing the 2% manual sample — plus real-time agent guidance. No rip-and-replace, 8 weeks to live.
talk_track.qualifying_question: Kieran mentioned the ops team has a view on QA prioritisation — is 100% coverage something you've been asked to solve, or is it more about agent coaching and consistency?
talk_track.ask: We've done the demo with Kieran and Viswa — can I bring that to the broader ops team? 90 minutes, on-site or remote.

objections.objection: We already have Salesforce Agentforce for this
objections.response: Agentforce is brilliant for CRM automation — it's not built for contact centres. Cresta is AI-native for CC: 100% QM on every call, real-time agent guidance in the moment. Forrester named us #1. We sit alongside Agentforce, not against it.

objections.objection: The data procurement process takes 3 months
objections.response: We don't need a data dump to start. We go live on real calls from week one and build the business case from actual interaction data. Woolworths did exactly this.
Viswa said the 3-year strategy is being defined right now. That's exactly the right time to set the CC AI standard — not retrofit it in year two.

cadence.action: Email Kieran — ops team demo booking
cadence.note: Attach Woolworths case study. Reference the 200-of-10,000 framing.
'Really valued your technical questions in the demo — keen to follow up on the Genesys integration piece.'

cadence.action: Phone Kieran if no email response
cadence.note: Ask directly: 'When can we get the ops team together?'

cadence.action: Email Robert Barnes (CTO) if Kieran stalls
cadence.note: Frame as extension of Microsoft AI partnership. CC Kieran on email.

cadence.action: Offer exec workshop if ops demo hard to convene

messages.subject: Amazon Connect + Salesforce at HL — Michael Loberman suggested you
messages.body:
Charlie,

Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person — you own the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap at HL.

Two things I'd like your view on:

1. Is Agentforce delivering the CC intelligence you need for agent coaching — or is it still primarily a CRM tool?

2. Under FCA Consumer Duty, how are you evidencing fair outcomes across pensions and investment conversations at scale? Manual QA on a sample doesn't evidence 100% — which is what the Duty requires.

We work with Mutual of Omaha on Amazon Connect — same stack, financial services, similar regulatory environment. 33% improvement in outcomes, QA cost halved in 6 months. I'd also mention that Declan Hourican will recognise our name from his time at TSB.

15 minutes? Happy to come to Bristol.

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi Charlie, Marcus Nolan from Cresta — Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person for a conversation about the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We sit on top of Amazon Connect and Salesforce — no replacement — and do two things: 100% call quality coverage for FCA Consumer Duty, and real-time guidance for agents during complex investment conversations. Mutual of Omaha runs exactly your stack — 33% improvement in outcomes in 6 months.
talk_track.qualifying_question: You're in the middle of the Amazon Connect transformation — is the AI coaching layer on the roadmap for this phase, or is it a separate workstream?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes with you and whoever owns QA and compliance monitoring? I'll bring the Mutual of Omaha case study and a specific Consumer Duty framework. Happy to come to Bristol.

objections.objection: We already have Agentforce — why do we need Cresta?
objections.response: Agentforce is excellent for CRM automation — built for that. Cresta was built specifically for the contact centre: 100% call QM, real-time agent guidance during the call, conversation intelligence. Forrester named us #1. We sit alongside Agentforce — not against it.

objections.objection: We're building our own retention AI
objections.response: Streatfield's build makes sense — you want to own that data asset. The question is: does it cover 100% call QM for Consumer Duty? Most teams building retention AI leave the compliance monitoring gap open. Cresta fills that gap — additive, not competitive.

objections.objection: Deloitte are already engaged on this
objections.response: Deloitte designs the strategy — we deliver the CC execution. We've worked alongside Deloitte on implementations before. If they're designing the AI roadmap, being in that conversation is worth a 15-minute call.

cadence.note: Reference Loberman. Mutual of Omaha attached. FCA Consumer Duty angle. Mention Hourican/TSB. Short — 3 paragraphs max.
Listen first. 'Tell me about what you're building in-house — where does Cresta accelerate it?'
Short personalised note — reference Loberman referral.

cadence.action: Follow-up if no email reply from Charlie
cadence.note: Phone call. Short — 'Michael Loberman suggested you. 15 minutes — I'll bring the TSB reference.'
Send retention signal detection + build vs buy comparison slides.

cadence.action: Route to Simon Belsham (CCO) if neither progressing
cadence.note: 'I've had good conversations with Charlie Peters and Matthew Streatfield — I think this is relevant for your CX agenda too.'
